title: Mortgage Calculator: Monthly Payment, Amortization & Total

schemas.name: Mortgage Calculator
schemas.description: Calculate monthly mortgage payments, see amortization schedules, and model fixed vs variable rates and extra payments.

mainEntity.name: What exactly is a mortgage calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: A mortgage calculator is a tool that estimates your monthly home loan payments based on the home price, down payment, interest rate, and loan term. It helps you understand the total cost of borrowing and how much you can afford to spend on a house.

mainEntity.name: How do I use a mortgage payment calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: Simply enter your desired home price, how much you plan to put down, your expected interest rate, and the term of the loan (e.g., 15 or 30 years). The calculator will instantly show your monthly principal and interest payment, as well as an amortization schedule.

mainEntity.name: How much is my mortgage payment going to be?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your mortgage payment depends primarily on the size of your loan, the interest rate, and the loan duration. Use our tool above to input your specific numbers and see the exact monthly payment breakdown for both standard and extra payment scenarios.

mainEntity.name: Why should I compare 15-year vs 30-year mortgages?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 15-year mortgage has higher monthly payments but saves you tens of thousands of dollars in interest and builds equity twice as fast. A 30-year mortgage offers lower, more manageable monthly payments but costs much more in interest over the life of the loan.

mainEntity.name: Can an extra $100 a month really make a difference?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes! Making extra payments towards your principal reduces the balance faster. For example, adding just $100 extra per month to a typical 30-year mortgage can shave several years off your loan and save you thousands of dollars in interest.
